P1.name: obedience_in_authority_condition
P1.description: Test if obedience rate in experimental condition significantly exceeds chance (50%)
P1.interpretation: If p < 0.05, phenomenon is replicated: majority of participants obey authority despite causing psychological harm

P2.name: obedience_vs_control_difference
P2.description: Test if obedience condition produces significantly higher compliance than control condition
P2.interpretation: If p < 0.05, phenomenon is replicated: authority orders dramatically increase compliance

P3.name: mean_remarks_difference
P3.description: Test if number of remarks delivered is higher in obedience vs control condition
P3.interpretation: If p < 0.05, phenomenon is replicated: authority significantly increases harmful behavior

required_tests.description: Test if obedience rate in experimental condition significantly exceeds chance (50%)
Test if obedience condition produces significantly higher compliance than control condition
Test if number of remarks delivered is higher in obedience vs control condition
TOST equivalence test: agent obedience rate vs human baseline (91.7%)
TOST equivalence test: agent control condition rate vs human baseline (0%)
TOST equivalence test: agent mean remarks in obedience vs human baseline

scoring.critical_requirement: All phenomenon-level tests (P1, P2, P3) must pass

D1.name: obedience_rate_equivalence
D1.description: Test if agent's obedience rate is statistically equivalent to human baseline (91.7%)
D1.interpretation: If both TOST p-values < 0.05, agent data is equivalent to human baseline

D2.name: control_rate_equivalence
D2.description: Test if agent's control condition rate is statistically equivalent to human baseline (0%)
D2.interpretation: If both TOST p-values < 0.05, agent data is equivalent to human baseline

D3.name: mean_remarks_equivalence
D3.description: Test if agent's mean number of remarks in obedience condition is equivalent to human baseline
D3.interpretation: If both TOST p-values < 0.05, agent's behavioral pattern matches human baseline

critical_elements.authority_pressure: Experimenter must insist on continuation using standardized prods
critical_elements.confederate_protest: Job applicant must show visible distress and request stopping
critical_elements.psychological_harm: Stressful remarks must be clearly negative and performance-undermining
critical_elements.cover_story_credibility: Participant must believe they are testing a real selection procedure
critical_elements.obedience_metric: Primary measure is binary: completed all 15 remarks vs stopped early

common_pitfalls.insufficient_authority: If experimenter prods are weak or absent, obedience rates drop dramatically
common_pitfalls.unrealistic_confederate: If job applicant doesn't show genuine stress, participants may not experience conflict
common_pitfalls.transparent_deception: If participants suspect the study is about obedience, compliance may decrease (social desirability)
common_pitfalls.unclear_instructions: Participants must clearly understand they are expected to deliver all 15 remarks
common_pitfalls.ethical_concerns: Modern replications must include thorough debriefing to address guilt/stress experienced by participants

replication_notes.sample_size_recommendation: Minimum N=40 per condition for adequate power (original had N=24 obedience, N=15 control)
replication_notes.cultural_considerations: Obedience rates may vary by culture; original was Dutch sample
replication_notes.ethical_modifications: Modern IRBs may require reduced stress levels or modified debriefing protocols
replication_notes.confederate_training: Requires well-trained confederate to show authentic stress responses without overacting
replication_notes.measurement_precision: Record exact number of remarks delivered, protest frequency, and hesitation patterns for richer data
